title: Noisy messages for index histogram
description:
## Bug Report

Noisy messages like this:

```json
{
  "level": "WARN",
  "time": "2025/10/08 18:10:37.342 +00:00",
  "caller": "stats_syncload.go:415",
  "message": "fail to get hist meta for this histogram, possibly a deleted one",
  "table_id": 1039,
  "hist_id": 2,
  "is_index": true
}
```

This happens for multiple table_id's and multiple hist_id's, but `is_index` is always true.

It looks like `mysql.stats_histograms` doesn't have an entry for the table_id/hist_id/is_index combination.

The message seems to repeat with the same properties.

This seems similar to:
- #57804
- #56740

v8.5.2
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2'), (3, NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'test%');
key_bug_features: Missing histogram entry for index in stats_histograms
